summary: R5 implementation (24 commits, G1-G8) landed with 453/473 Rust passing + 17 Vitest + 7 napi smoke. Coverage vs pass-2 gate holds: 21/21 Phase-1 error codes fired, 8/8 invariant quartets complete, 6/6 compromises with regression (Compromise #7 bin-gating is documented but has no current-state pin), 11/11 attack vectors have adversarial tests, 14/14 proptests landed. Public-API coverage climbed further: all of Engine::{create/update/delete}_{node,edge}, edges_from/to, register_subgraph, register_crud, call, call_as, trace, transaction, snapshot, handler_to_mermaid, grant_capability, revoke_capability, read_view, read_view_with, create_view, metrics_snapshot have direct or integration tests. Pass-2 finding F2's call_async gap is now OBSOLETE — call_async was replaced at G7 by call_as/call_with_revocation_at. Pass-2 F3 (free-fn vs module version API) closed: only pub mod version exists now. Pass-2 F1 (3 deferred proptests) and F4 (B3 Vitest coverage) partially persist. All 20 remaining Rust failures are legitimately R4b-deferred: 4 IVM rebuild-equivalence (R3 test-authoring — rebuild() stub has no backend source; Phase-2 when event-log lands), 11 grant-backed caps (testing::with_policy_allowing_revocation / subject_with_no_read_grants / UcanBackend / minimal_write_handler / iterate_write_handler all Phase-2 per compromise #1 and #4), 2 version_current (depend on Phase-2 version-chain IVM wiring through engine), 1 change_event_attribution_fields (cross-crate actor_cid/handler_cid/capability_grant_cid wire-through deferred — G5 mini-review widened ChangeEvent but population is Phase-2), 1 option_a_existence_leak + 1 ucan_stub_internal (Phase-2 grant-backed). No critical findings; 4 new minor gaps + 2 pre-existing pass-2 items still open.

coverage_stats.public_apis_covered: ~120 / ~130 (engine public API grew by ~20 methods vs pass-2; 10 napi methods still have zero Vitest: call_as, count_nodes_with_label, create_view, delete_edge, emit_event, grant_capability, ivm_subscriber_count, open_with_policy, read_view, revoke_capability)
coverage_stats.compromises_regressed: 6 / 7 (Compromise #7 `[[bin]]` required-features gating has no regression test; Cargo.toml still uses `test = false, bench = false` without `required-features` per compromise documentation — state is unchanged and deliberately so, but no test pins it)
coverage_stats.proptests_landed: 14 / 14 (3 pass-2-deferred still deferred per M16: prop_capability_check_deterministic, prop_hlc_monotonic, prop_transform_grammar_fuzz_accepted_deterministic)

pass1_disposition_map.pass1_finding_engine_crud_untested: closed (engine_crud.rs + engine_api_surface.rs at pass-2; G7/G8 implementation made them green)
pass1_disposition_map.pass1_finding_engine_integration_scaffol: closed (engine_api_surface.rs covers register_subgraph/call/trace/transaction/snapshot/grant+revoke/create_view; handler_to_mermaid covered via DSL layer + exit_criteria)
pass1_disposition_map.pass1_finding_ivm_views_1_2_5_shallow: closed (rewritten to 3-category coverage; rebuild-matches-incremental exists but currently fails — see r4b-rtc-4)
pass1_disposition_map.pass1_finding_version_chain_free_fn_drif: closed (only pub mod version exists in current lib.rs; top-level free fns removed)
pass1_disposition_map.pass1_finding_e_cap_attenuation_not_fire: closed (call_attenuation.rs at pass-2 — though the test is now failing for Phase-2 reasons; see r4b-rtc-3)
pass1_disposition_map.pass2_finding_f1_three_deferred_proptest: still-open (documented R5 deferral; now post-R5-implementation and still not landed — see r4b-rtc-1)
pass1_disposition_map.pass2_finding_f2_call_async_direct_test: obsolete (call_async method removed at G7; replaced by call_as + call_with_revocation_at, both of which DO have direct tests in engine_api_surface.rs and toctou_iteration.rs)
pass1_disposition_map.pass2_finding_f3_version_api_drift: closed (crate-root free fns removed; only pub mod version remains)
pass1_disposition_map.pass2_finding_f4_b3_vitest_coverage: still-open (10 napi methods have zero Vitest coverage — see r4b-rtc-2)

findings.location: workspace — 3 proptests missing that triage M16 deferred to R5
findings.claim: Three proptests that R4 triage M16 explicitly deferred to R5 implementation remain absent after G1-G8 complete.
findings.detail: Search for `fn prop_capability_check_deterministic`, `fn prop_hlc_monotonic`, `fn prop_transform_grammar_fuzz_accepted_deterministic` across crates/ + bindings/ returns zero hits. Triage R4 disposition said 'land during R5'; R5 is done. (a) `prop_capability_check_deterministic` is natural in benten-caps alongside NoAuth/UCAN backends — currently absent. (b) `prop_hlc_monotonic` has no HLC harness yet (Phase 3 CRDT plumbing), so legitimately deferred again to Phase 2/3. (c) `prop_transform_grammar_fuzz_accepted_deterministic` — the TRANSFORM parser landed in G6, the grammar rejection surface is tested (40 rejections in transform_grammar_rejections.rs), but the determinism proptest over accepted expressions is still absent.
findings.suggested_fix: R4b: add (a) and (c) now that the impl is green — prop_capability_check_deterministic over NoAuth (trivially ok-unconditional, already covered by prop_noauth_returns_ok_unconditionally — rename/merge if that IS the intended coverage) and prop_transform_grammar_fuzz_accepted_deterministic against the accepted-expression corpus. (b) HLC defer to Phase 3 is legitimate; document in triage.

findings.location: bindings/napi/index.test.ts + packages/engine/src/*.test.ts — 10 napi methods not touched by Vitest
findings.claim: 10 of 25 napi-exported Engine methods have zero Vitest assertions. Pass-2 F4 remained open pending G8; G8 is now complete but the TS tier still lacks coverage for these methods.
findings.detail: grep of `engine.{method}` across all Vitest files shows these napi methods have zero TS-side calls: `call_as` (callAs), `count_nodes_with_label` (countNodesWithLabel), `create_view` (createView), `delete_edge` (deleteEdge), `emit_event` (emitEvent), `grant_capability` (grantCapability), `ivm_subscriber_count` (ivmSubscriberCount), `open_with_policy` (openWithPolicy), `read_view` (readView), `revoke_capability` (revokeCapability). Rust-side coverage exists for most via engine_api_surface.rs / engine_crud.rs — the gap is only at the TS tier. Note: delete_edge is called Rust-side only; deleteNode is Vitest'd but deleteEdge isn't.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one Vitest per uncovered napi method in bindings/napi/index.test.ts. `grantCapability` + `revokeCapability` + `readView` are the highest-value (exit-criterion #3 and #6 adjacencies); `createView`, `callAs`, `emitEvent` are adjacent to handler execution; the rest are thin passthroughs acceptable as smoke-coverage only.

findings.location: .addl/phase-1/ + docs/SECURITY-POSTURE.md — Compromise #7 has no regression test
findings.claim: Named Compromise #7 (`[[bin]]` required-features gating deferred to Phase 2 for benten-graph::write-canonical-and-exit) has no regression test pinning the current deliberately-under-gated state. Other 6 compromises each have a named test in compromises_regression.rs.
findings.detail: crates/benten-graph/Cargo.toml:28-39 declares `[[bin]] name = "write-canonical-and-exit"` with only `test = false, bench = false`. The compromise doc (plan §R1-Triage-Addendum row 7) says Phase 2 adds `required-features = ["test-fixtures"]` gating. No test in compromises_regression.rs (6 tests, covering #1-#6 only) or elsewhere fires on the Cargo.toml state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `compromise_7_benten_graph_bin_gating_is_phase_2_deferred` to compromises_regression.rs that reads crates/benten-graph/Cargo.toml, asserts the [[bin]] section exists with `test = false, bench = false`, and asserts it does NOT have `required-features`. This lets the regression fail loudly when Phase 2 applies the gate, forcing test-update-in-lockstep with the compromise closure. Also reference Compromise #8 (closed at G7 pass-2 per R4b brief) — if it warranted closure, it should either get a 'compromise_8_closed' marker test or be removed from docs.

findings.claim: 4 IVM rebuild-equivalence tests fail because `rebuild()` in each of Views 1/2/3/5 is implemented as 'clear state, reset budget, clear stale flag' with NO source to rebuild from — the impl is Phase-1 stub. The tests assume rebuild reads authoritative state from the backend/event-log. This is the R3 test-authoring bug the R4b brief calls out. Coverage gap: the rebuild semantics the test asserts are Phase-2 (event-log replay or full backend scan); no current test accurately pins the Phase-1 rebuild contract.
findings.detail: Inspection of capability_grants.rs:285-295 (similar in event_handler_dispatch.rs:276, version_current.rs:238, governance_inheritance.rs:289): rebuild() clears state and returns Ok. The test `view1_rebuild_matches_incremental_state` constructs `incremental` (populated via events) and `rebuilt` (populated only by rebuild()), then asserts payload equality — rebuilt is empty, incremental is non-empty, test fails. Phase-1 rebuild is a NO-OP from populated state perspective. R3 author mis-modeled semantics. content_listing has a `rebuild_from_scratch` method that IS non-no-op, but the `View::rebuild` trait impl at line 370 calls the same clear-and-reset. The proptest `prop_content_listing_incremental_equivalence` triggers the same mismatch.
findings.suggested_fix: Two options. (A) Rename tests to `viewN_rebuild_clears_state_and_resets_stale` and assert the Phase-1 contract (rebuild is a reset, not a replay). Add TODO(phase-2): 'rebuild replays from backend / event-log when G5-follow-up lands'. (B) Fix rebuild() to accept a seed iterator — requires threading benten-graph NodeStore iteration into the View trait; larger change, Phase-2-natural. Recommend (A) for R4b to unblock the 4 failures while preserving coverage intent; (B) during Phase-2 IVM completion.

findings.claim: Major R1 attack vector #9 (causal attribution on every evaluation) has a coverage test that currently fails. The test asserts actor_cid / handler_cid / capability_grant_cid fields on ChangeEvent are populated during a handler write. G5 mini-review widened the ChangeEvent struct (commit 8a19445) but Engine::call does not populate those fields through the PrimitiveHost put_node path.
findings.detail: ChangeEvent struct at benten-graph has fields actor_cid, handler_cid, capability_grant_cid (Option<Cid>). Engine writes go through PrimitiveHost::put_node → backend.put_node. The wire-through to populate those fields from the active-call state (lib.rs:327 'Active Engine::call stack') is not implemented. Test is correctly spec'd; impl is Phase-2-adjacent. This test is the ONLY regression preventing silent drift on attack vector #9 — leaving it red at gate-close means a passing future could erode the contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) land the wire-through in R4b since Engine already tracks active-call metadata in the struct at lib.rs:333; ~30 LOC to read from self.active_call and stamp the ChangeEvent at put_node time. (b) Mark the test `#[ignore = "phase-2 causal attribution wire-through per R1 major #9"]` and add to an R6 pre-gate fixlist. Recommend (a) — the infrastructure exists, only the populate-at-emit site is missing.

findings.claim: Two version_current integration tests (version_current_o1_resolution_at_every_step, version_current_updates_synchronously_with_append) are failing. These exercise the IVM View5 through the Engine, but the underlying Anchor wire-through in benten-core is intentionally Phase-2 deferred (lib.rs:642 version-chain API).
findings.detail: Tests assume Engine::create_node of Anchor + append_version updates View5 synchronously. Phase-1 plan reconciliation says 'version chains as opt-in pattern, not primitive' — Engine doesn't walk them automatically. The failing tests conflate Phase-2 synchronous-IVM-maintenance-of-version-chains with Phase-1 opt-in walkability.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `#[ignore = "phase-2 engine-level version-chain IVM wiring"]` gate + a TODO(phase-2) doc line. Rename to `version_current_o1_resolution_when_engine_manages_anchors` to make the deferral explicit. The current in-crate benten-core::version::append_version + walk_versions tests DO pass (anchor_version.rs, version_branched.rs) — the Phase-1 opt-in contract is covered there; the engine-integration-level O(1)-resolution is the deferred piece.

findings.location: Multiple napi exports lack Rust-side unit tests for the napi-wrapping layer itself
findings.claim: napi exports call through to Engine::{method} — the Rust-side Engine tests cover the logic, but the `#[napi]`-macro-generated translation layer (serde_json::Value → typed args → napi::Result<Outcome>) has no dedicated smoke test per method. If an argument deserialization bug lands (e.g. serde mismatch on grant_capability's serde_json::Value argument at bindings/napi/src/lib.rs:290), no Rust-side test fires; only runtime Vitest would catch it (and pass-2 F4 shows 10 napi methods have no Vitest).
findings.detail: The fully-unexercised combination: `grant_capability`, `revoke_capability`, `call_as`, `emit_event`, `create_view`, `read_view` napi methods have neither Rust-side napi-layer tests nor Vitest coverage. bindings/napi/tests/input_validation.rs tests input-limit enforcement (5 tests) but not these methods specifically.
findings.suggested_fix: Address via r4b-rtc-2 fix (add Vitest for each). Deeper fix: when napi-rs v3 supports `#[test]` on `#[napi]` methods natively, add per-method serde roundtrip tests. Phase-1-acceptable minor.

failure_disposition_map.toctou_iteration::capability_revoked_mid: phase-2-deferred (Phase-2 grant-backed revocation API; testing::with_policy_allowing_revocation + schedule_revocation_at_iteration + iterate_write_handler)
failure_disposition_map.ucan_stub_messages::ucan_stub_error_rout: phase-2-deferred (depends on Engine + UcanBackend wire-through; UcanBackend stub exists but Engine's eval→UcanBackend routing is Phase-2)
failure_disposition_map.integration::compromises_regression::com: phase-2-deferred (grant-backed revocation harness; compromise doc is accurate, only test harness is Phase-2)
failure_disposition_map.integration::exit_criteria_all_six::exit: phase-2-deferred (grant-backed; exit-criterion #3 has Vitest mirror that may cover differently)
failure_disposition_map.requires_enforcement::handler_cannot_esc: phase-2-deferred (requires-property enforcement at call-time is Phase-2; currently Phase-1 only validates structural requires at registration)
failure_disposition_map.view3_content_listing::prop_content_list: R3-test-authoring-bug (see r4b-rtc-4; the trait impl rebuild clears; rebuild_from_scratch exists as inherent method but not wired to View trait)

notes: All 20 failures are legitimately deferred: 13 require Phase-2 grant-backed capability harness (testing::subject_with_no_read_grants, with_policy_allowing_revocation, iterate_write_handler, UcanBackend call-routing wire-through, requires-property call-time enforcement) — these are consistent with Phase-1 plan §R1-Triage-Addendum which explicitly defers per-operation cap checks to Phase-2 Invariant-13. 4 are IVM rebuild-equivalence test-authoring bugs where rebuild() is correctly a Phase-1 no-op reset but tests assume Phase-2 event-log-replay semantics. 2 are version-chain engine-level IVM integration deferred. 1 (change_event_attribution_fields) is fixable in R4b with ~30 LOC if Ben wants to close that R1 attack-vector gap proactively. Verdict 'accept-with-minors': none of the 7 new findings are critical, and pass-2 gate coverage (21/21 errors, 8/8 invariants, 14/14 proptests, 11/11 attack vectors, 6/7 compromises regressed) holds. The 6/7 on compromises is the only near-miss (Compromise #7 lacks a pin test — r4b-rtc-3); fix is a 10-line Cargo.toml-reading test.
